body:

I just got off the phone with my campaign manager and had to let you know the good news immediately: we hit our end-of-month goal!

You are always up for the challenge, and I am both proud and grateful to have your support. Thank you so much! We continue to show that, when we all come together, we truly can do amazing things, my friend.

I hope you have a wonderful week, and please always know that Team Sri values your continued support.

Thank you again,

Sri Preston Kulkarni

Mike will not only fight for the people, but he has the backbone to stand against the big donors and corporate interests now running roughshod over us. He’ll truly be the people’s representative in Washington." Our goal is to run a true people’s campaign: no funding from corporate PACs, lots of support from regular folks who want a representative who will fight for them. By reaching out to voters across the diverse urban, rural, and suburban counties that make up the Texas 10th, we've turned this solid red district to one of the most competitive battlegrounds in the country. body:
President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump "arrived at Buckingham Palace Monday just after noon, local time, to meet Queen Elizabeth." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4f80540380&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **_Queen Elizabeth Greets President Trump at Start of State Visit_**

President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump "arrived at Buckingham Palace Monday just after noon, local time, to meet Queen Elizabeth," Maria Puente reports in _USA Today_.

“The queen greeted President Trump after he ascended the palace stairs with Prince Charles at the West Terrace of Buckingham Palace. Melania walked behind with Duchess Camilla. The queen wore a big smile as she greeted the president with a handshake.”

President Trump’s three-day state visit to the U.K. is timed to coincide with the 75th anniversary of D-Day.

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d3c1a6d5f3&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “President Trump was in Virginia Sunday for a special church service praying for victims of the Virginia Beach mass shooting,” Mark Moore and Lee Brown report in the [_New York Post_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3979d8f363&e=3ee4985eea). “Trump stood behind pastor David Platt as he offered a prayer for the 12 killed in Friday’s mass shooting.”
---
|
---
| “The Trump administration Friday announced approval of year-round access to gasoline with higher blends of ethanol, a move that could boost corn prices for farmers and lower costs for drivers,” Donnelle Eller writes in the [_Des Moines Register_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2fafb67773&e=3ee4985eea). “The decision is welcome news for farm groups that have pushed for expanded consumer access to gasoline with 15% ethanol.”
---
|
---
| “Mueller and his predecessors spent two years investigating, with full cooperation from the White House. Nadler knows perfectly well that his ‘investigations’ won’t find anything new,” the [_New York Post_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=13e48ff8fe&e=3ee4985eea) editorial board writes. “It’s nothing but an elaborate stall, and Mueller is entirely right to not waste his time in a pointless, political dog-and-pony show.”
---
|
---
| “Sheriffs on the front lines of the surging illegal immigration crisis are pushing back against pro-sanctuary advocates” who oppose helping Immigration and Customs Enforcement officials detain criminals, Paul Bedard reports in the [_Washington Examiner_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d1e6ddf32c&e=3ee4985eea). “Soon, every county is going to be a border county,” warned Sheriff Chuck Jenkins of Frederick County, Maryland.
---
|

subject: I want to say thank you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger For Congress
body:
Thank you, thank you, Peter. When my finance team and I went over our final fundraising numbers this weekend, I was incredibly thankful and humbled.
| | Thank you, thank you, Peter.

When my finance team and I went over our final fundraising numbers this weekend, I was incredibly thankful and humbled. Hundreds of individuals in VA-07 and across the country stepped up to support us so that we could meet our fundraising goal for the month of May.

**Today, I want to say thank you to each and every member of this inexhaustible grassroots community:** It's the direct result of your kindness, generosity, and persistence that we won in 2018 and are well positioned for 2020 at these early stages. I am deeply motivated to continue working for the people of Central Virginia. You are a true example of the power of civic action in our world today.

Both on the ground and in the halls of Congress, May was an incredible month for our campaign. We took big steps to lower the price of prescription drugs, support small businesses owned by service families, and expand high speed internet to every household and business in our district.

**If we keep fighting like this, Peter, I have no doubt that these big steps will bring big results to Central Virginia.**

Peter, thank you again for everything: the energy you've committed to our campaign, the dollars you've donated, and the time you've spent hearing us out in these emails. I'm so excited to see what we accomplish in June and beyond.

_\-- Abigail_

name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: Oklahoma
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BETO FOR AMERICA
body:
[1]Beto for America

Yesterday we went to Oklahoma to meet with residents who are recovering
from severe flooding. Volunteers from all across the country have come in
to help. Neighbors and families are doing everything they can to pitch in
and look out for each other, showing the very best of the American spirit.

In the town of Sand Springs, I met Jeremy, who warned all his neighbors to
prepare after looking at the early water levels, the historical data, and
doing the math himself for how high it'd rise this time. The Army Corps of
Engineers put out official warnings just hours before some of the worst
floods hit the area. His wife, Kim, told us that a lot of their neighbors
don’t have social media or regular access to the Internet. They were
counting on someone telling them they needed to evacuate but that never
happened. No one ever came by. Now both Kim and Jeremy are left wondering
why we couldn’t have done a better job notifying residents earlier.

Jeremy talks to us about mapping the flood plain to know how high the
water will go.

Right across the street, we met with the family of a World War II Air
Force veteran, Chester. His house ended up with four feet of water inside.
But when the water started pouring in, Kim ran across the street to pull
the clothes out of his drawers and stuff them into garbage bags, grabbing
anything she could for Chester.

When Chester’s great niece Jamie called him to check in, he said he
couldn’t wait on FEMA and that he was going to start cleaning up now. So
Jamie and her husband got in the car, went to get some supplies, and made
the drive over where we met them as they were helping tear the damage out.

Some possessions are replaceable, but many are not. Among the possessions
sitting outside on the front yard was a flooded WWII trunk with Chester’s
uniforms, medals, letters, and keepsakes from the war. They were hoping it
could be saved but knew some of it couldn’t be.

Jamie, in the blue shirt, shows us Chester’s house.

Jamie shows us the damage to Chester’s World War II chest.

We heard a few things from every person we talked to on the ground.

First, that the place we visited, Town and Country, is such a special
community — from the neighbors helping neighbors to the family members
taking off work to help people pack up belongings, clean up from the
damage, and try their best to prepare for the storms that will return
later this week. We came away so impressed by the sense of dedication
these Oklahomans have to one another.

Second, we heard concerns from almost everyone about the lateness of the
warning system. Jeremy told us Congressional action is needed to give the
Army Corps the authority to notify people earlier based on forecasts. We
have to find a way to disseminate that information as early as possible.
Some neighbors told me they didn’t think Congress knows what's going on in
their community but those living in the neighborhoods impacted did. We
need to do a better job putting communities and those on the ground in the
driver’s seat.

Third, residents need much faster recovery response times. They can’t
afford to take out expensive loans while they wait for disaster money. It
can’t take months for people to be able to get basic resources, like it
did in Puerto Rico. Much of the responsibility lies with Congress. Kim
told me that one neighbor just gave up and moved out of the neighborhood
-- with no plans to return because they don’t want to wait on the
resources to come in.

County Commissioner Karen Keith mentioned a FEMA program that puts a
trailer in your yard for 18 months, connecting to your existing utilities.
That helps keep people rooted in their neighborhoods. Fewer people end up
selling their homes for cheap, which helps limit the economic damage to
the neighborhood.

Commissioner Karen Keith shows us damage to the levy.

We also heard a lot about flood planning — that it’s important for our
federal government to prepare for disasters before they hit. That’s why
our climate plan calls for significant investments in pre-disaster
mitigation grants. Local communities need the resources to build up the
infrastructure and the levees to prevent the worst damage before it
happens.

From the beginning, we’ve said our campaign would be about showing up
everywhere for everyone. That’s why it was so important for us to be there
in Oklahoma with people who are going through this difficult time. I want
to make sure that no matter where you live, we can come and listen to you,
learn from you, and incorporate your stories into the campaign we’re
running.

We are so grateful to all those who came up to say hello and talk to us in
Oklahoma on Sunday. Those who helped us become better informed. We’ll be
thinking of these communities in the days to come and want to make sure
they get the resources they need. Our job now is to take in these insights
and stories. What we learned will be invaluable as we seek to serve
Oklahoma and the rest of this country.

Volunteers who came in to help residents in Sand Springs.

Before I go, I want to ask all of you to consider chipping in to the Tulsa
Community Foundation’s disaster relief fund. The funds they collect will
aid the residents of eastern Oklahoma in their long-term recovery. You can
chip in here: [ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/17624?t=1001&akid=34326%2E444211%2E8dMlI1 ]https://tulsacf.org/?fund=1409

- Beto

| Friend,
Another week campaigning across the state is in the books. From the coast to north Mississippi and back. It's always exciting to see where the trial takes us and to hear the concerns of Mississippians first hand.
We've spent a great deal this month traveling across this great state and one thing we know is certain - people are excited at the opportunity to elect someone who knows what it's like to meet payroll.
Moving forward, we have an exciting and full week ahead campaigning. Friday is the second Gubernatorial debate at Southwest Mississippi Community College, and I am looking forward to once again taking the stage on your behalf. When we hire someone in business, we ask them for an interview. And this election should be no different. We owe it to the voters of Mississippi to let them know where we each stand on the important issues we all face together.

We continue to deliver large 4'x8' Foster signs across the state. If you would like to help us get our message out by sponsoring a large sign on a Mississippi Highway or commercial property, [donate here!](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001NqKSlvAwQauSukMKk1uPH_ZDMcUPapFJObqvL5F-B0s4vS1wUpeCP4FQ1zg0cAsx4eMLyZ6c36tIY6Xu3OpRjVuBAlPoDZnY493GfC0KiplTKWhOoGjY-VupTkp8EjStOR0Uaeg9i0WPptJYAipbkQzV2z4z_wCiJ83jIm4nmMk=&c=ppfaNmz-fyFZMbftYPap0joDZq_jCJ41m2zlxAAqfpJfPXQ8jTNhaw==&ch=F8d2ORRthRTriie9ukYF_VrB5OnBtmdGq88wZ24ua9RdzkzwPZeblA==)
The support you all have shown us and our message of the political outsider that is resonating with so many make the end result that much more worth it every week!

Team,
It takes a lot of money to run a successful Senate campaign. That creates a lot of pressure to spend time at fundraisers and on the phone with wealthy donors.
That's not how I'm hoping to spend my time. We won our campaign for mayor of Helena by knocking on doors and connecting with one voter at a time. I know that if I can spend my time traveling to every corner of Montana and talking with voters, we can win this race, too.
[**That 's where you come in, team: can you contribute $5 right now to help keep me off the phones and meeting with voters?**](https://act.wilmotcollins. When campaigns are funded primarily by special interest donors, it skews candidates' priorities. Republican Steve Daines' number one source of funding is Wall Street--and as a senator, he's made choices that put corporate interests ahead of Montana families.

[**Contribute $5 now to power our campaign and elect the Democratic representation we need.**](https://act.wilmotcollins. Wilmot

name: Tina Smith
subject: This is a long email on mental health, but I hope you'll take a moment to read it
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
This is personal for me.

| [](https://action.tinaforminnesota. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-email-june?refcode=rtw_20190603_em&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=") Friend, When I first came to the Senate, I knew I wanted to make mental health one of my top priorities. Millions of Americans struggle with mental health issues, and they deserve our help. As I spoke about mental health care with people, it started to feel weird that I wasn't just saying, "Hey, I'm relating to this personally" \-- and that's why I decided to share my own experience with depression. I experienced it initially in my late teens, and then again in my 30s. Both times, I'd started finding it harder and harder to cope with the daily challenges of life. The things that used to give me joy didn't always anymore, and the things I used to love to do just made me exhausted. I wasn't living my best life. In fact, I wasn't really living at all. That's the reality of mental illness for millions of Americans. And I'm one of the lucky ones. I was lucky that my college had excellent mental health resources and my roommate cared about me enough to urge me to take advantage of them. And I was lucky that, when my depression came back in my 30s with a vengeance, I had health insurance that covered treatment. I chose to share my story because I want to urge anyone who struggles with depression -- or anxiety, or substance abuse, or post-traumatic stress disorder, or any other mental health issue -- to reach out and seek help. I know firsthand that seeking help can be scary. There is no happily ever after when it comes to mental illness -- but happier is possible. But de-stigmatizing and de-mystifying mental illness is just the beginning. And I'm writing to you because I want to do more: **I 've introduced a bill to expand mental health services in our schools -- so we can expand access to care for students and help reduce the stigma that surrounds mental health care.** **[Today, I'm asking you to sign on as a citizen co-sponsor to prioritize mental health services in this country. By adding your name, you're sending a clear message that we're not backing down in this critical fight.](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4f320/c1f7015/636c7018/114733cf/2924915833/VEsE/ "https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/s/rtw_201906_mentalhealth?source=rtw_20190603_em")** **[ADD YOUR NAME](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4f320/c1f7015/636c7018/114733cf/2924915833/VEsF/ "https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/s/rtw_201906_mentalhealth?source=rtw_20190603_em")** Across our state and our country, we're seeing a shortage of mental health care providers, and we're seeing insurance companies fail to fully cover mental health services. **One thing is certain: Regardless of your insurance, zip code, or age, everyone should have access to help and mental health care.** My own experience showed me how important getting help can be -- and I won't stop fighting until we make it easier for others to seek and receive care, too. **[Before midnight, add your name to sign on as a citizen co-sponsor to prioritize mental health services in this country. We have a real opportunity to change people's lives, and I'm asking you to join this fight.](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4f320/c1f7015/636c7018/114733cf/2924915833/VEsC/ "https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/s/rtw_201906_mentalhealth?source=rtw_20190603_em")** Thank you for standing with me, Tina | **U.S. Senator Tina Smith is running for re-election in 2020 in a race _NBC News_ has already labeled a "race to watch" with Donald Trump targeting Minnesota for victory. Make sure she has the resources to fight back.** [CONTRIBUTE](https://action.tinaforminnesota. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tsm-email-june?refcode=rtw_20190603_em-biobox&amount=10&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=&recurring=auto")

clean_body: This is personal for me. | "=" Friend, When I first came to the Senate, I knew I wanted to make mental health one of my top priorities. Millions of Americans struggle with mental health issues, and they deserve our help. As I spoke about mental health care with people, it started to feel weird that I wasn't just saying, "Hey, I'm relating to this personally" \-- and that's why I decided to share my own experience with depression. I experienced it initially in my late teens, and then again in my 30s. Both times, I'd started finding it harder and harder to cope with the daily challenges of life. The things that used to give me joy didn't always anymore, and the things I used to love to do just made me exhausted. I wasn't living my best life. In fact, I wasn't really living at all. That's the reality of mental illness for millions of Americans. And I'm one of the lucky ones. I was lucky that my college had excellent mental health resources and my roommate cared about me enough to urge me to take advantage of them. And I was lucky that, when my depression came back in my 30s with a vengeance, I had health insurance that covered treatment. I chose to share my story because I want to urge anyone who struggles with depression -- or anxiety, or substance abuse, or post-traumatic stress disorder, or any other mental health issue -- to reach out and seek help. I know firsthand that seeking help can be scary. There is no happily ever after when it comes to mental illness -- but happier is possible. But de-stigmatizing and de-mystifying mental illness is just the beginning. And I'm writing to you because I want to do more: I 've introduced a bill to expand mental health services in our schools -- so we can expand access to care for students and help reduce the stigma that surrounds mental health care. "" "" Across our state and our country, we're seeing a shortage of mental health care providers, and we're seeing insurance companies fail to fully cover mental health services. One thing is certain: Regardless of your insurance, zip code, or age, everyone should have access to help and mental health care. My own experience showed me how important getting help can be -- and I won't stop fighting until we make it easier for others to seek and receive care, too. "" Thank you for standing with me, Tina | U.S. Senator Tina Smith is running for re-election in 2020 in a race NBC News has already labeled a "race to watch" with Donald Trump targeting Minnesota for victory. Make sure she has the resources to fight back. ""

name: Castro 2020 Policy Team
subject: The system is broken
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Julian for the Future
body:
| | |

---
| | In the United States, **nearly 1,000 people** are shot and killed by police each year. Sadly, you don't have to do the math to know that a disproportionate number of black men are the victims of excessive police force. This is not a case of a few bad apples. **The system is broken. ** There are far too many stories like that of Stephon Clark, an unarmed 22-year-old who was gunned down by the police in his own grandmother's backyard. We can't become numb to these incidents or accept them as normal. It's time to make real change through policy that protects and serves all Americans. As president, Julian would demand a federal response.
[**This is Juli an's plan to fix this broken system**](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcdd1/3eb3e2fc/410d4d52/74494375/2390522088/VEsE/ "http://www.julianforthefuture.com/news-events/people-first-policing/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=2+-+This+is+Juliaacutenrsquos+plan+to+fix+th&utm_campaign=20190603_em_pol-policing-rollout&source=20190603_em_pol-policing-rollout"): **1\. Introduce new policing guidelines.** **2\. Hold police accountable.** **3\. Start the healing process between communities and law enforcement. ** [](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcdd1/3eb3e2fc/410d4d52/74494374/2390522088/VEsF/ "https://www.julianforthefuture.com/news-events/people-first-policing/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=3&utm_campaign=20190603_em_pol-policing-rollout&source=20190603_em_pol-policing-rollout") The release of **[Julian's policing reform plan](https://action.julianforthefuture.com/page/m/709fcdd1/3eb3e2fc/410d4d52/74494373/2390522088/VEsC/ "https://www.julianforthefuture.com/news-events/people-first-policing/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=juliancastro&utm_content=4+-+Juliaacutens+policing+reform+plan&utm_campaign=20190603_em_pol-policing-rollout&source=20190603_em_pol-policing-rollout")** follows his People First Immigration and Education platforms, which have set the standards for immigration and education policy among 2020 Democratic contenders. All politicians must step out of their comfort zones to make necessary change for the good of our nation. The loss of innocent lives must stop, and comprehensive policing reform is the first step.

subject: In photos: Queen Elizabeth II welcomes President Trump and the First Lady
body:
From Buckingham Palace to Westminster Abbey, President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump kicked off their State Visit... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7dc0ea2a93&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • June 3, 2019
---
| | |
# **In photos: Queen Elizabeth II welcomes President Trump and the First Lady**

**From Buckingham Palace** to Westminster Abbey, President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump kicked off their State Visit to the United Kingdom with a trip to some of the U.K.’s most significant landmarks.

**The historic visit began** with a royal welcome as Marine One landed on the lawn of Buckingham Palace, where Charles, Prince of Wales, and Camilla, Duchess of Cornwall, greeted the President and First Lady. Queen Elizabeth II welcomed President Trump with a handshake at the top of the palace stairs.

**This week’s trip** reflects the special relationship between America and Great Britain, coinciding with the 75th anniversary of D-Day. On June 6, 1944, the Allied forces landed on Omaha Beach in France in the largest land, air, and sea invasion in military history. Thousands of soldiers from both nations lost their lives, but their courage led to victory in World War II—and thus changed the course of history.

**Commemorating** this shared sacrifice, British armed forces played an important role in today’s ceremonies. After the performances of both the American and British national anthems, President Trump accompanied Prince Charles for a traditional inspection of the uniformed Guard of Honor.

**At Westminster Abbey,** Prince Andrew, the Duke of York, served as a guide through the church’s historic halls. While inside, the President and First Lady stopped to lay a wreath at the Grave of the Unknown Warrior. Set in the central aisle of the Abbey, the tomb represents all those who died in service but have no known grave, much like Arlington National Cemetery’s own Tomb of the Unknown Soldier.

**This evening,** Queen Elizabeth concluded the day’s busy schedule of events with an official State Banquet. From Buckingham Palace's ballroom, members of the Royal Family—along with some 170 guests—welcomed America’s First Family.

[In photos: President Trump and the First Lady arrive at Buckingham Palace.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3ddd4bf936&e=3ee4985eea)

**Watch:**[Queen Elizabeth leads a tour through the Royal Collection display](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6ab82c036e&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **Honoring the victims of Virginia Beach**

**On Friday afternoon,** America mourned the loss of 12 souls after a gunman opened fire in a senseless act of violence at a municipal building in Virginia Beach, Virginia.

**“Our Nation** grieves with those affected by the tragic shooting,” President Trump said in a Proclamation for the victims. “Americans unite in praying for God to comfort the injured and heal the wounded.” In honor of those victims, he also ordered the U.S. flag to be flown at half-staff at the White House and other public grounds.

[Read President Trump’s Proclamation.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0486812d7a&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|

body:
With the first debates just around the corner, we want to know how you feel. | [ ](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/29319413/1cd0ac28/259caa1c/356c98dc/1494695934/VEsH/ "https://www.joebiden.com")
---
Peter, the 2020 election will be the most important of our lifetime.

And if we are going to elect Joe Biden, we need up to the minute, accurate data from our supporters.

That's why we're sending you our brand new June Debate Strategy Survey.

**Peter, you have been selected to represent voters in Cresco. Please complete the survey by midnight to ensure accurate results:** | | [**BEGIN SURVEY »**](https://go.joebiden.com/page/m/29319413/1cd0ac28/259caa1c/356c98db/1494695934/VEsE/ "https://go.joebiden.com/page/s/June-Survey?source=email-bsd-20190603june-b2_SRV_NS_NA-F1_S1_C1&utm_medium=email&utm_source=bsd&utm_campaign=20190603june&utm_content=b2_SRV_NS_NA&utm_term=F1_S1_C1")

**Your input helps us accomplish that. So please Peter, take our June Debate Strategy Survey right away:**

Team Joe

name: Ben, Cofounder of Ben & Jerry's
subject: You haven’t claimed your free bumper sticker yet
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bernie 2020
body:
These are going fast.

# [](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/8915?t=1&akid=1570%2E150750%2EVKHw1U)
Friends -
First, thanks so much for being part of the political revolution that we’ve needed for so long, _and is finally within our grasp._
I just got back from several big rallies with Bernie on the West Coast and l can tell you that they were packed and Berniecrats are psyched!
I’m one of the National Co-Chairs of Bernie’s campaign. That may sound highfalutin, but most of what I’ve been working on is...BUMPER STICKERS!
That’s because this is a grassroots campaign. And hundreds of thousands of bumper stickers can demonstrate a **_visible groundswell of support for Bernie_** that will continue to snowball.
[**Sign up today to receive your FREE Bernie bumper sticker.**](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/bernie-2020-sticker/?source=em190603-2-45&refcode2=1570.150750.VKHw1U&t=2&akid=1570%2E150750%2EVKHw1U)
[](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/bernie-2020-sticker/?source=em190603-2-45&refcode2=1570.150750.VKHw1U&t=3&akid=1570%2E150750%2EVKHw1U)
[GET YOUR STICKER](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/bernie-2020-sticker/?source=em190603-2-45&refcode2=1570.150750.VKHw1U&t=4&akid=1570%2E150750%2EVKHw1U)
Right now it is my sworn duty to get 500,000 of these stickers on bumpers around the country.
The reason bumper stickers are so powerful is that **when you put one on your car, it’s your personal, visible endorsement.** Anyone who’s got the money can buy a TV ad, but nobody can buy your personal endorsement. People understand that. And that’s why it’s so impactful.
So please, tell the world (at least the part of it that is behind your car) that you support Bernie for president of the United States.
[**Add your name today to tell us where to send your free sticker.**](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/bernie-2020-sticker/?source=em190603-2-45&refcode2=1570.150750.VKHw1U&t=5&akid=1570%2E150750%2EVKHw1U)
In solidarity,
Ben Cohen
Cofounder, Ben and Jerry’s
King of Bumper Stickers, Bernie 2020

Paid for by Bernie 2020
PO BOX 391, Burlington, VT 05402
This email was sent to **dpwillis67@gmail.com**. If you need to, [**unsubscribe here**](https://bernie.actionkit.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=6&akid=1570%2E150750%2EVKHw1U). You can also [sign up to receive fewer emails](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/fewer-emails/?t=7&akid=1570%2E150750%2EVKHw1U). We hope you'll stay, because emails are such an important part of how we organize and how we raise money. In fact, there is no single greater source for our campaign's fundraising than emails like this one. And that's important, because no one person, not even Bernie Sanders, can take on Trump and the billionaire class alone.

Before you go about the rest of your day, help power our fight against the billionaire class by [making a contribution today](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-email-template?refcode=em-footer&t=8&refcode2=1570_150750_VKHw1U&akid=1570%2E150750%2EVKHw1U).
Submit this form to [update your contact information](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/update-info/?t=9&akid=1570%2E150750%2EVKHw1U).
